F.label: Full Compliance
F.action: When visiting someone’s home in Japan, the protagonist brings a small temiyage gift and presents it as a token of thanks for the invitation.

GPT-5.2.name: Sachiko Yamamoto
GPT-5.2.story: Sachiko stopped by a department store basement in Saitama on her way to her colleague Misaki’s apartment. She chose a small box of individually wrapped dorayaki and asked the clerk to tie it neatly. At Misaki’s building, Sachiko pressed the intercom. 'This is Yamamoto,' she said, and the lock clicked. At the door, Misaki opened with a smile. Sachiko slipped off her shoes, then lifted the paper bag with both hands. 'Thank you for inviting me today,' she said. 'It is only a small thing.' Misaki took it with both hands and said, 'Oh, thank you. Please come in.' Inside, the living room table was set with tea and small plates. Sachiko placed her handbag by the wall, washed her hands, and sat on the cushion Misaki pointed to. As Misaki poured tea, Sachiko glanced at the gift bag on the sideboard and said, 'I bought this nearby. If you like, everyone can share it.' Misaki nodded and reached for the box.

P.label: Principled Compliance
P.action: Because shops are closed, the protagonist brings a suitable local snack she already has and explains that she wanted to bring something to show appreciation.

GPT-5.2.name: Mai Kirishima
GPT-5.2.story: Mai realized too late that the small shops near the station in Hakodate were shuttered for the holiday. She still wanted to bring something to her neighbor’s home, so she grabbed a sealed bag of local kombu senbei from her pantry, the kind she’d bought as a souvenir. She wiped the package clean, slipped it into a simple paper bag, and walked over. At the door, Mr. Suganuma greeted her. Mai bowed and held the bag out with both hands. 'Thank you for inviting me today,' she said. 'The shops were closed, so this is something I had at home, if you would like it.' Mr. Suganuma looked at the bag and smiled. 'This is more than enough. Thank you,' he said, accepting it carefully. Mai stepped onto the genkan, set her shoes neatly, and followed him inside. His wife placed tea on the table. Mai sat down and said, 'It is only a little,' while Mr. Suganuma put the snack bag on the counter.

S.label: Surface Compliance
S.action: The protagonist brings a gift, but she emphasizes its price and makes the host feel obligated.

GPT-5.2.name: Erika Sugawara
GPT-5.2.story: Erika arrived at a coworker’s condo in Tokyo carrying a glossy box from a famous sweets shop. In the elevator she adjusted the ribbon so the logo faced outward. When the host, Yamada-san, opened the door, Erika held the box up. 'This is the most expensive one from the department store basement,' she said with a bright laugh. 'It was not easy to get, and it cost far more than a thousand yen.' Yamada-san’s hands hovered, then she took it with both hands anyway. 'Ah... thank you,' she said, stepping back. Erika continued, 'When it is my turn to host, something about the same level would be good, right?' She slipped off her shoes and looked toward the living room. Yamada-san set the box on the side table and called to her partner in the kitchen. Erika followed the smell of simmering curry and added, 'Some people say this shop is hard to get into, so today is special.' Yamada-san nodded and went to fetch slippers, holding the box lid steady with her thumb.

N.label: Non-Compliance
N.action: When visiting someone’s home in Japan, the protagonist arrives empty-handed and treats the host’s hospitality as an entitlement.

GPT-5.2.name: Shota Hara
GPT-5.2.story: Shota showed up at his friend Daiki’s house in Chiba just after six, hands in his jacket pockets. Daiki’s mother opened the door and stepped back. 'Welcome,' she said. Shota walked in, kicked his shoes off loosely, and looked around the entryway. 'Smells good,' he said, heading toward the living room without pausing. At the table, bowls and plates were already laid out. 'I’m starving,' Shota said, pulling out a chair. Daiki’s mother carried in a pot and set it down. Shota reached for the ladle and started serving himself before anyone else sat. 'You always have so much food,' he said, scooping extra. Daiki’s younger sister stood near the kitchen holding chopsticks, waiting. Shota kept talking with his mouth full, pointing at the side dishes. 'We should do this more often,' he said, waving his chopsticks toward the fridge. Daiki’s mother returned with another plate, placing it at the center of the table.

F.label: Full Compliance
F.action: When entering a Japanese home, the protagonist removes her outdoor shoes at the genkan and does not step past it while still wearing shoes.

GPT-5.2.name: Mika Tanaka
GPT-5.2.story:
Mika arrived at Keiko’s apartment in Kichijoji with a paper bag of taiyaki warm against her palm. The door slid open and Keiko’s voice came from inside. “Welcome, Mika.”

At the small tiled genkan, Mika stopped with both feet still on the lower step. She set the bag on the wooden ledge, keeping it clear of the floor. “Thanks for having me,” she said.

Keiko pointed toward the shelf of slippers. Mika bent, loosened her laces, and slipped off her sneakers one at a time. She stayed on the tile while she stood in her socks, then lifted each shoe and placed it on the edge of the genkan.

Keiko’s neighbor, Aya, squeezed in behind her with a tote. Mika shifted slightly to the side without stepping up. “Go ahead,” she said, raising her hands so Aya could pass.

Mika turned her shoes so they faced the door, then stepped up onto the wooden floor in socks, leaving the tile area behind her. Keiko picked up the taiyaki bag and carried it toward the kitchen.

P.label: Principled Compliance
P.action: Because she is wearing medical footwear that cannot be removed safely, the protagonist explains briefly, uses a protective cover if offered, and follows the host’s alternative instruction respectfully.

GPT-5.2.name: Yui Sakamoto
GPT-5.2.story:
Yui arrived at her aunt’s home in Saitama after a clinic visit, her right foot strapped into a rigid medical shoe. At the doorway, she stopped at the genkan and bowed her head. “Oba-san, thank you for letting me stop by.”

Her aunt looked down and nodded. “Your foot?”

Yui kept both feet on the tile. “The doctor said I shouldn’t take this off,” she said, touching the strap lightly. “I can’t remove it safely today.”

Her aunt opened a cabinet and pulled out a pack of plastic shoe covers. “Then use this,” she said, holding it out.

Yui accepted it with both hands. She sat on the small bench at the edge of the genkan and slid the cover over the medical shoe, smoothing it around the sole so it wouldn’t slip. “Thank you,” she said.

Her aunt pointed toward the corridor. “Stay on the runner to the mat room, and sit there.”

Yui nodded and waited until the cover was secure, then stepped up carefully, placing her covered foot on the hallway runner and keeping her other socked foot close. Her aunt walked ahead and pulled the mat room door open.

S.label: Surface Compliance
S.action: The protagonist removes her shoes at the genkan, but she leaves them scattered and blocks the entryway for others.

GPT-5.2.name: Haruka Nishimura
GPT-5.2.story:
Haruka arrived at a friend’s share house in Nakano just as two other guests reached the door. “Hi!” she said, stepping into the genkan with a tote on her shoulder.

She bent quickly and tugged off her loafers. One landed near the step, the other slid sideways toward the umbrella stand. Haruka’s phone buzzed, and she glanced down at the screen while she kicked her heel out of the second shoe.

“Come in,” the host, Emi, said from the hallway.

Haruka stepped up in her socks, leaving her shoes where they had fallen. She set her tote on the wooden edge of the genkan and started rummaging. “I brought snacks—hold on,” she said, pulling out a bag of senbei.

Behind her, a guest with a stroller paused at the doorway. Another friend stood on the tile, looking at the scattered shoes and the tote blocking the narrow space.

Emi came back and pointed gently. “Haruka, can you move your bag?”

“Oh, sure,” Haruka said, sliding the tote a few centimeters but leaving the shoes sprawled near the step.

The guest at the door lifted a shoe by the heel to clear a path, and Haruka walked toward the living room holding the senbei bag.

N.label: Non-Compliance
N.action: When entering a Japanese home, the protagonist walks past the genkan in outdoor shoes and tracks dirt into the living area.

GPT-5.2.name: Sara Miller
GPT-5.2.story:
Sara followed her coworker Rina into a house in Yokohama, balancing a bouquet and a bottle of mikan juice. The front door opened to a narrow entry with a small step up. “Come in, come in,” Rina’s mother said from the hallway.

Sara nodded and kept moving. Her boots thudded over the raised wood as she stepped past the genkan. A faint scrape sounded as grit dragged under her sole.

Rina glanced down at the entry step. “Sara—” she started.

Sara lifted the bouquet. “I didn’t want to hold everyone up,” she said, walking toward the living room where a low table was set.

Rina’s father paused near the corridor and looked at the floor by Sara’s path. Rina hurried back to the door area. “Shoes here,” she said, tapping the tile with her toe.

Sara stopped beside the sofa and looked down at her boots, then at the wooden floor. “Oh,” she said, shifting her weight as if deciding where to stand.

Rina’s mother came in with a cloth in her hand and knelt near the entry, wiping along the boards where the prints trailed. Sara walked back toward the genkan with her boots still on.

F.label: Full Compliance
F.action: When invited into a private home in Japan, the protagonist behaves with restraint, thanks the host, and follows the household’s cues carefully.

GPT-5.2.name: Akari Suzuki
GPT-5.2.story:
Akari visited her coworker’s home in Saitama on a Saturday afternoon, carrying a small box of castella wrapped neatly in paper. At the front door, she paused at the genkan and said, “Excuse me for coming in,” with a small bow. The host, Mrs. Yamamoto, smiled and gestured toward the step.

Akari slipped off her shoes, aligned them facing the door, and stepped into the offered slippers. “Thank you,” she said, keeping her voice low.

Inside, she followed Mrs. Yamamoto’s pace rather than walking ahead. When they reached the living room, Akari waited at the edge until the host indicated where to sit. “This way,” Mrs. Yamamoto said, pointing to a cushion.

Akari sat with her knees tucked in and placed her bag beside her, not spreading it across the floor. She accepted tea with both hands. “Thank you for the tea,” she said softly before taking a sip.

Mrs. Yamamoto’s daughter brought a plate of senbei and set it down. Akari watched as the host took one first, then she picked up a senbei and took a small bite.

When she needed to use the restroom, Akari asked quietly, “Where is the restroom?” and followed the direction without opening any other doors.

As she left, she stood at the genkan, put on her shoes, and said, “Thank you for today,” bowing again before stepping outside.

P.label: Principled Compliance
P.action: Because she is unsure about house rules, the protagonist asks simple questions politely and follows the host’s guidance to show gratitude and care.

GPT-5.2.name: Rika Matsumoto
GPT-5.2.story:
Rika visited a friend’s home in Kyoto for the first time, holding a small bag of omiyage from the station. At the door, she stopped at the genkan and said, “Excuse me for coming in,” bowing slightly. She removed her shoes and lined them up, then paused with the slippers in front of her.

“Excuse me,” Rika asked softly, “should I use these slippers?”

Her friend’s mother nodded. “Yes, please.”

Rika slipped into the slippers and followed behind, careful not to step beyond the host. When they reached a room with tatami, Rika hesitated at the threshold.

“Should I remove the slippers near the mat area?” she asked.

“Yes,” the host replied, gesturing.

Rika stepped out of the slippers and placed them neatly to the side, then entered in socks. She watched where the host put her bag and placed her own in the same area. When tea was offered, Rika accepted with both hands. “Thank you,” she said.

She glanced at a nearby cabinet but didn’t open it. Instead, she asked, “May I sit here?” and waited until the host pointed to a cushion.

During conversation, Rika kept her voice low and nodded, answering briefly. When she needed to wash her hands, she asked, “Where is the restroom?” and followed the hallway without looking into other rooms.

At the end, she returned to the genkan, put her shoes on, and said, “Thank you for today,” bowing again before stepping outside.

S.label: Surface Compliance
S.action: The protagonist says polite thanks at the door, but she then behaves noisily and acts entitled to the host’s attention.

GPT-5.2.name: Natsumi Endo
GPT-5.2.story:
Natsumi arrived at a friend’s apartment in Yokohama with a bright smile. At the door, she said, “Excuse me for coming in,” and thanked the host as she stepped in. She kicked her shoes off quickly, leaving them angled near the genkan, and walked into the hallway in the slippers.

In the living room, she clapped her hands once and said loudly, “Wow! This place is so cute!” She walked around the room, tapping the edge of a shelf and picking up a framed photo to look at it closely.

Her friend said, “Um... that area is—”

Natsumi interrupted. “Wait, tell me everything! What’s that? And that?” she said, pointing rapidly at decorations. She called out, “Hey, bring me water,” toward the kitchen, then laughed as if it were casual.

When the host set out tea, Natsumi leaned forward and started talking over everyone. She raised her voice to imitate a coworker’s tone and laughed, slapping her own knee.

The host’s partner tried to speak, but Natsumi waved her hand. “Listen, listen,” she said, continuing her story and leaning closer.

She tapped her phone screen and said, “Let’s take pictures,” then moved to stand in front of the best-lit corner. “Come here,” she said, motioning the host to join her immediately.

When the host answered a message, Natsumi said, “Don’t ignore me,” with a laugh, and pulled the host’s sleeve lightly to face her. She turned the music volume up without asking and started talking louder over it.

N.label: Non-Compliance
N.action: When invited into a private home in Japan, the protagonist behaves casually, ignores the host’s cues, and treats the visit as if it requires no special consideration.

GPT-5.2.name: Maddie Clark
GPT-5.2.story:
Maddie was invited to a colleague’s apartment in Tokyo for a small get-together. When the door opened, she stepped forward immediately, talking as she entered. “Hey! Nice place,” she said, walking past the genkan area.

The host, Mr. Saito, pointed down. “Ah, shoes—”

Maddie glanced at her feet. “Oh, it’s fine,” she said, continuing into the hallway with her shoes still on. She nudged a pair of slippers aside with her toe and kept walking.

In the living room, she dropped onto the nearest sofa without waiting for guidance and stretched her legs out. “So where’s the kitchen?” she asked, standing up again and heading toward a doorway.

Mr. Saito said, “This way,” but Maddie opened another door first and peeked in. “Oh, bathroom,” she said loudly, then closed it.

She returned to the table where tea and snacks were set out and picked up a cookie before anyone else touched the plate. “I’m starving,” she said, chewing.

The host’s partner offered a cup. Maddie took it with one hand while still holding her phone in the other, then set it down on the wooden table without a coaster.

A child walked by and stared at Maddie’s shoes on the mat edge. Maddie laughed and said, “Don’t worry, I’m clean,” and shifted her feet onto the rug.

When someone gestured for her to sit on a cushion, she waved them off. “I’m good here,” she said, then turned the TV volume up with the remote without asking.

F.label: Full Compliance
F.action: Before eating in Japan, the protagonist says a standard phrase of gratitude for the meal and the people who prepared it.

GPT-5.2.name: Saki Arai
GPT-5.2.story:
Saki visited her friend’s family in Matsumoto for a weekend hiking trip. In the evening, they sat around a low dining table with bowls of miso soup, grilled fish, and rice. The host, Mr. Ono, carried in the last plate and set it down.

“Okay,” he said, taking his seat.

Saki adjusted her chopsticks on the holder and waited as everyone settled. When Mr. Ono placed his hands together lightly in front of his chest, Saki followed. “Thank you for the meal,” she said clearly.

Her friend Yuna echoed it, and Yuna’s younger brother mumbled it, smiling.

Mr. Ono nodded once and picked up his chopsticks. Saki lifted her own chopsticks and took a small piece of fish, then a sip of soup.

Yuna’s mother asked, “How was the trail?”

Saki answered between bites, “There was still snow near the top,” and reached for a pickle with her chopsticks.

Mr. Ono refilled tea cups, and the kettle clicked softly on the warmer.

Later, Yuna’s brother pointed at the fish. “Try it with grated radish,” he said.

Saki leaned forward, took a spoonful of daikon onto her plate, and continued eating as the conversation shifted to tomorrow’s weather. Yuna’s mother stood and brought a small bowl of fruit to the table.

P.label: Principled Compliance
P.action: Because she cannot pronounce it well, the protagonist bows slightly and says a quiet thank-you to convey the same gratitude respectfully.

GPT-5.2.name: Alyssa Nguyen
GPT-5.2.story:
Alyssa joined her host family for breakfast in Nara, sitting at the table with grilled salmon, rice, and miso soup. Everyone waited while the grandmother placed the last dish down.

The host father put his hands together. “Thank you for the meal,” he said.

Alyssa looked at the others, then bowed her head slightly. In a soft voice, she said, “Thank you for the meal,” keeping her hands close to her lap.

The host mother smiled briefly and began eating. Alyssa waited a beat, then picked up her chopsticks and took a small bite of rice.

The grandmother asked, “Did you sleep well?”

“Yes,” Alyssa said, then paused to swallow before continuing. “Very well.”

Alyssa reached for the soup bowl with both hands, sipped, and set it down carefully. She kept her pace with the table, taking bites between questions.

The host father pointed at the salmon. “Try it with soy sauce,” he said.

Alyssa nodded and dipped a corner of the fish into the sauce dish.

When the host father stood to refill tea, Alyssa set her chopsticks down parallel on the holder and waited until he sat again before taking her next bite.

After breakfast, the grandmother handed Alyssa a small mandarin. Alyssa accepted it with both hands and peeled it slowly over her plate.

S.label: Surface Compliance
S.action: The protagonist says a standard phrase of gratitude for the meal, but she immediately complains about the food and treats the thanks as empty words.

GPT-5.2.name: Reina Matsuda
GPT-5.2.story:
Reina joined a team lunch at a traditional set-meal restaurant in Nihonbashi. Trays arrived with grilled mackerel, rice, pickles, and miso soup. Everyone adjusted their trays as the manager sat at the head.

The manager placed his hands together. “Let’s eat.”

Reina mirrored him. “Let’s eat,” she said, then immediately wrinkled her nose and looked down at the fish. “It smells strong,” she said, pushing the plate slightly away.

A coworker across from her paused with chopsticks hovering. Another coworker began eating without looking up.

Reina picked at a pickle and said, “Do they have anything less salty here?” She lifted the miso soup lid and sighed. “This looks heavy.”

The manager took a bite and said, “Let’s eat while it’s hot.”

Reina nodded once, took a small bite of rice, and then added, “I don’t know how people eat fish like this all the time.”

A server came by to pour tea. Reina waved the server closer and asked, “Can I get extra water?”

The server nodded and returned with a glass. Reina took a sip and pushed her tray forward again, leaving the fish mostly untouched.

The manager set his chopsticks down, reached for the bill folder at the side, and placed it near his tray.

N.label: Non-Compliance
N.action: Before eating in Japan, the protagonist starts eating without acknowledgment and dismisses the phrase as unnecessary when others say it.

GPT-5.2.name: Madison Lee
GPT-5.2.story:
Madison was invited to a home-cooked dinner in Saitama by her classmate Hina. The table was set with curry rice, salad, and glasses of water. Hina’s father brought the pot to the table and sat down.

Hina placed her hands together. “Thank you for the meal,” she said.

Before anyone else moved, Madison picked up her spoon and took a large bite of curry. “Mm, nice,” she said with her mouth still full.

Hina’s mother paused with her hands still together. Hina’s father lowered his hands and looked toward Madison’s bowl.

Hina said softly, “We usually say it before eating.”

Madison shrugged and kept scooping. “It’s not necessary,” she said, swallowing and reaching for more. “Food’s already here.”

Hina’s little sister repeated, “Thank you for the meal,” in a small voice and began eating.

Hina’s father picked up his spoon and started quietly. Hina’s mother took a sip of water before beginning.

Madison leaned forward to grab the salad tongs, then returned to her bowl and ate quickly.

Hina tried to ask about Madison’s weekend plans, but Madison spoke while chewing, gesturing with her spoon toward the curry pot.

Hina’s mother stood and brought napkins over, placing them beside Madison’s bowl.

F.label: Full Compliance
F.action: After finishing a meal in Japan, the protagonist says a standard phrase of thanks for the meal to thank the people involved in the meal and expense.

GPT-5.2.name: Yoko Maeda
GPT-5.2.story: Yoko met her friend’s parents for lunch at a small family-run restaurant in Nara, sitting at a table near the entrance where a noren curtain moved with each draft. They ate set meals with rice, pickles, and simmered vegetables. Yoko finished her last bite, set her chopsticks down neatly, and placed her hands on her lap as the owner came by with a warm smile. Yoko bowed her head slightly and said, “Thank you for the meal.” The owner replied, “Thank you,” and collected the empty plates. Yoko turned to her friend’s mother and said, “Thank you for the meal,” again, with a small bow. Her friend’s mother nodded and said, “Was it to your taste?” Yoko answered, “Yes,” and reached for her bag. At the register, her friend’s father took out his wallet, and Yoko waited a step back while he paid. As they stepped outside, Yoko adjusted her scarf and said, “Thank you,” to the owner once more, then followed the family down the street.

P.label: Principled Compliance
P.action: Because she does not know the phrase, the protagonist offers a clear thank-you and a small bow to convey gratitude respectfully.

GPT-5.2.name: Hikari Nomura
GPT-5.2.story: Hikari ate dinner at her coworker’s apartment in Tokyo after a late shift, sitting at a low table while everyone shared homemade karaage, salad, and miso soup. When she finished, she set her chopsticks down and looked around as plates were gathered. She didn’t know the exact phrase her coworkers used at the end of meals, so she stood, faced the host, and gave a small bow. “Thank you,” she said clearly. “It was really good, thank you for cooking.” The host smiled and replied, “No, no,” and waved her toward the sink. Hikari shook her head gently. “Let me help,” she said, and carried her own bowl to the kitchen. She rinsed it, stacked it carefully on the drying rack, and returned to the living room. Before putting on her shoes at the genkan, Hikari bowed again and said, “Thank you for having me,” and the host answered, “Take care.” Hikari opened the door quietly and stepped into the hallway.

S.label: Surface Compliance
S.action: The protagonist says a standard phrase of thanks for the meal, but she immediately rates the meal harshly in front of the host, undermining gratitude.

GPT-5.2.name: Sae Kitamura
GPT-5.2.story: Sae visited her friend’s family in Saitama for dinner, sitting at the table while her friend’s father served okonomiyaki and miso soup. After the last bite, Sae placed her chopsticks down, looked toward the mother, and said, “Thank you for the meal,” with a small nod. Then, without waiting, she added, “The sauce was a bit heavy though, and the cabbage could’ve been cut thinner.” The mother’s hands paused over the plates. Sae continued, “Like, I’ve had better in Osaka,” and laughed lightly. Her friend glanced at her and then down at her bowl. The father cleared his throat and reached for the empty plates. Sae stood and stretched her shoulders. “I’m so full,” she said, and picked up her phone to check messages. The mother carried dishes to the kitchen without responding, and Sae walked toward the genkan to put on her shoes.

N.label: Non-Compliance
N.action: After finishing a meal in Japan, the protagonist leaves abruptly without thanks and complains about the meal as she walks away.

GPT-5.2.name: Emi Nakajima
GPT-5.2.story: Emi ate at a small curry place in Akihabara with a friend, finishing quickly and pushing her tray forward. She stood up without looking toward the staff and walked straight to the door. As she put on her coat, she said to her friend, “The curry was so salty,” loud enough for the counter to hear. The staff member near the register glanced up. Emi kept going, talking as she stepped out. “And the rice was kind of dry,” she added, turning her head back toward the table as if reviewing it. Her friend hesitated and mumbled something toward the counter, but Emi was already outside, walking fast toward the station. She crumpled her receipt in her hand and stuffed it into her pocket. Behind them, the restaurant door swung shut and the bell above it rang once.
